meta.note: ged_new covers the 25 x86 sailr projects only (cps/malware have no source CFGs).

cat1b_recoverable_via_merge_fix.note: Degenerate cached CFGs whose REAL body exists in a captured .i but was clobbered by the nondeterministic last-writer-wins merge (proto stub wins). PROVEN: zlib deflate=1 node cached / 176 fresh; e2fsck_pass1=1/443.

cat4_decompiled_parse_failure.note: Decompiler produced a real (non-Loading) function body but Joern returned no CFG for it (source CFG present). Per-function (Joern recovers neighbors).

breakers.angr_phoenix_ghidra: aggregate/array return type rendered as 'T [N] name(...)' (ghidra: 'undefined1 [16]') -> Joern parses nothing. CONFIRMED.
breakers.binja: 'arg @ rax' register annotations in the signature -> Joern parses nothing (CONFIRMED); plus ~538 'other' that parse fine ALONE but are dropped in the whole-file parse (error-recovery cascade among @reg-malformed neighbors).
breakers.ida: '__int128' params/returns -> Joern parses nothing (CONFIRMED); plus multi-underscore name-normalization mismatches and whole-file effects.

cat5_source_parse_failure.note: Probe of largest .i per project. Header-stripping already fixed the old timeouts.

cat2_proven_example_shadow_main.reason: GED scores nologin's decompiled main (a 5-node body) against a 56-node main from a different shadow program; merge is nondeterministic (imap_unordered).
